mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of May 2018 reflects post-crisis accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% as of May 2018 indicates labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, signaling weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412403 million dollars as of May 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite weak retail sales signal, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, indicating economic slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances with INDPRO declines exceeding 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth and unemployment rate move in opposite directions: rising payrolls combined with declining unemployment signal robust labor market strength that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.15% AND UNRATE declines more than 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133852 thousand as of May 2018, UNRATE at 7.7%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline while inflation accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year signals stagflationary pressure: rising costs reduce housing affordability and consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 894 thousand units as of April 2018, CPIAUCSL at 231.025 as of May 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or interaction Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling monetary policy lag and real rate compression that initially supports equity valuations but creates tail risk of policy tightening, producing positive but volatile S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in this specific regime.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 231.025 as of May 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances meeting both conditions, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while retail sales remain flat or decline, signaling late-cycle demand exhaustion and recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth is negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13638.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2018, RSAFS at 412403 million dollars as of May 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or interaction Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.
